name: skills-live-in-both-main-and-dev-worktree
tags: skills directory, main worktree, dev worktree, git sync, port stray edits, skill.md, task:b648c1d1
body:
skills/*.md files exist in both ~/workspace/claude-hooks (main) and ~/workspace/claude-hooks-dev (dev) as separate git checkouts of the same repo — editing one does not update the other. Deployed copy at ~/.claude/skills/ must also be synced manually (cp).

Why: skills aren't part of the hook-server dev→test→main pipeline (that's for hooks/*.py, langchain_learning/*, dispatcher.py), so direct edits to skills in main don't flow through /deploy. Precedent commit 95b4b61 ("port stray direct main edits back into dev") shows this has caused drift before.

How to apply: when editing a skill.md, write to main, copy to ~/.claude/skills/ (deployed), and copy to claude-hooks-dev too, committing all three locations (two git commits, one plain file copy) so the worktrees don't diverge again.
